extracted_codes.context: First, the Biobank dataset was screened for presence of ICD9/ICD-10 codes for rheumatoid arthritis
First, the Biobank dataset was screened for presence of ICD9/ICD-10 codes for rheumatoid arthritis

clinical_criteria: At least one RA diagnosis code required for inclusion
PPV = 0.946 and NPV = 0.923 in training set
At least 1 joint with definite clinical synovitis (swelling) for target population
Score of 6/10 needed for classification using 2010 ACR/EULAR criteria
RF positive = 1, RF negative or no test = 0 for Lab_RF feature
algorithm_summary: This RA algorithm uses a machine-learning logistic regression model (LASSO) trained on chart-reviewed cases based on 2010 ACR criteria. The algorithm includes multiple features: RA_COD_DX_RheumatoidArthritis_v2 (RA diagnoses), RA_COD_DX_SystemicLupusErythematosus (lupus diagnoses), RA_COD_DX_PsoriaticArthritis_v2 (psoriatic arthritis diagnoses), RA_COD_LAB_RFpos1 (rheumatoid factor), and Patient_dxenct (total diagnosis encounters). Each feature has an associated beta coefficient weight. The model calculates a predicted probability using: exp(Intercept + sum_beta)/(1 + exp(Intercept + sum_beta)) where Intercept = -1.017. Patients with predicted probability >= 0.632 are classified as RA cases. Controls are subjects without RA diagnosis codes and without any control exclusion codes. The algorithm achieved AUC = 0.95, with performance validated in independent test sets at Harvard and Vanderbilt.
